message:
test(ipc): end-to-end Cx-first transport integration tests (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add a new integration test file documenting the full ft-xbnl0.2.3
Cx-first IPC transport pipeline. Supplements unit-level cx tests
landed in ticks 102+103 with end-to-end stack coverage — useful for
the bead's acceptance-criteria evidence matrix (item #3: concurrent
clients, disconnects, shutdown).

Three tests exercise the full pipeline:

1. `ipc_cx_first_bind_run_ping_shutdown_roundtrip` — bind_with_cx →
   run_with_cx → client Ping → server Pong → shutdown via legacy
   channel → verify socket removed. Confirms the Cx-first pipeline
   is wire-protocol-compatible with legacy IPC.

2. `ipc_cx_first_cx_cancel_alone_tears_down_server` — bind_with_cx
   → run_with_cx → never send shutdown signal → cancel caller's cx
   → verify accept loop tears down in <2s and socket file is
   removed. Demonstrates the behavioral upgrade from pure-signal
   shutdown to cx-first cancellation (tick 103's root helper change).

3. `ipc_cx_first_multiple_concurrent_clients` — 4 concurrent
   clients each complete a Ping/Pong roundtrip without interference.
   Pins that the cx-threaded accept loop does not serialize
   connections or drop any under concurrent load.

Uses the standard `[cfg(all(unix, feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]`
gate and routes shutdown sends through `runtime_compat::mpsc_send`
helper for cross-runtime compatibility.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 3/3 tests
pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
